waypoints.narration: The patriarch of Western music. Bach's mastery of counterpoint and harmony influenced every generation that followed.
Bach's second son bridged Baroque and Classical styles, his Empfindsamer Stil emphasizing emotional expression.
The youngest Bach son brought Italian operatic elegance to London, influencing the young Mozart.
Beethoven expanded the symphony and sonata beyond all previous bounds, carrying Bach's structural rigor into the Romantic era.
Mendelssohn revived Bach's music for the 19th century, conducting the St Matthew Passion's first performance in a hundred years.
Schumann championed new music through criticism and composition, linking Beethoven's legacy to the next generation.
Brahms completed the lineage — a Romantic who composed with Classical discipline, revered as Bach's spiritual heir.
